nts2.n: HIT A LICK RADIO w/ ALACARTE MUSIC
nts2.d: Chester, PA’s DJ FINNAHITALICK (Blackwealth in the club) is a seasoned sound archivist of Underground Hip-hop. Starting Hit A Lick Radio in 2016, DJ FINNAHITALICK explores rap’s extensive subgenres and regional epicenters, ranging from DMV crank and Atlanta Plugg to Milwaukee low-end, Michigan punch-rap and beyond. His fast-paced mixes encapsulate Underground Hip-hop’s diverse landscape, and are not for the weak-hearted.
Native Sound System join us once a month, breaking the latest sounds from Lagos

Imagine Soundtracks w/ redkoffee: Wings of Desire (R)
NTS x DistroKid: City Signal w/ Lilith

subtle.d:
Sound Arkitek is a Chicago hip-hop DJ with a love for all things drum & bass. 

With over 20 years experience playing house parties, raves, clubs and bars he has an encyclopedic knowledge of music but has always been a participant in jungle culture. In the early 2000s he was exposed to the genre primarily through Dieselboy and Danny The Wildchild mixes as well as the “Jungle Book” series of mixtapes. He became obsessed after hearing Bad Company’s “The Nine” and “Four Days,” as well as Pendulum’s “Vault.” 

As time went on, he got deeper into the scene, going to raves while buying records and posting on Drum & Bass Arena and DNBForum before turning 21 at which point he began going to Lava Lounge for Bass By The Pound’s “Seminar” series. The first legal drum & bass party he attended was Chase & Status at the aforementioned Lava Lounge, and he will never forget the energy when the duo played DJ Hazard’s “Killers Don’t Die” and it got a rewind. His love for the scene led to him playing drum & bass parties, opening for artists like Original Sin, Gridlok, and LTJ Bukem among others. 

His Subtle Radio show is an hour of the finest tunes in the scene at the moment. 

To keep up, follow @djsoundarkitek on Instagram!

Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.
Potent Funk records’ Matt Frost - Well known for his technical abilities and Sleazy bass music style, Frost covers all angles from UK alt Rap to the LA beat scene.

Get ready to embark on a captivating sonic journey of underground music with Charlie Kelly. Including new and timeless sounds from the depths of rap, drill, grime, and experimental genres from the vibrant streets of the UK and beyond. Charlie unearths the very best in music that pushes boundaries. Off the mic, Charlie is a Radio Producer and Social Media Executive with an eclectic music taste spanning across many different cultures, decades, and styles. Hailing from North London with a love for audio and pop culture, you can expect to hear tune after tune when you’re locked into Charlie Kelly on Subtle.
BirdE brings an hour of the freshest Drum and Bass covering everything from liquid to techy steppers - the deeper the better.

Originally from Cambridge, Counter Culture's selection deftly navigates across a myriad of influences and exclusives from himself & friends.
Expect some of the freshest rolling soul straight to your headspace.

subtle.d: ixxi and Fei take you on a percussive journey with their show syncop8, moving from downtempo ambient to breaks, polyrhythms, and high energy beats, exploring every rhythmic and textural nuance in between.
Representing UK Bass Music Culture across a range of styles, sounds and tempos.

dublab.j: Seeing It As You Really Are

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy

dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
